basics.name: Iliya Liampert
basics.label: Political Science Ph.D. Student
basics.summary: Ph.D. student in Political Science at Michigan State University specializing in American Politics, Congress, the Presidency, and Racial and Ethnic Politics. Research focuses on legislative representation, minority political behavior, and diversity in executive appointments. Experienced in quantitative methods, survey experiments, and machine learning.

education.summary: Subfield: American Politics, Congress, Presidency, Racial and Ethnic Politics.
Advanced research methods, institutional decision-making, game theory, homeland security, political theory, Israeli politics, American politics.

education.institution: Vytautas Magnus University / European Humanities University
education.summary: Thesis: Race/Ethnic and Sexual Minorities’ Political Struggle in the United States in the late 2010s: Preconditions, Tactics, and Effects

work.name: Public Policy and Social Research (IPPSR), Michigan State University
Senate Votes Project
work.summary: Co-authoring and coordinating research examining congressional voting patterns.

publications.name: President Biden and Diversity in Executive Appointments

projects.name: Who Gets To Govern? Diversity in Executive Appointments
projects.description: Working paper examining determinants of diversity in U.S. executive branch appointments.

projects.name: Coded Campaigns: The Rhetorical Strategies of Minority Candidates
projects.description: Working paper analyzing how minority candidates employ coded and strategic rhetoric.

awards.title: College of Social Science Provost Undergraduate Research Initiative Award
awards.summary: $2000 with Ian Ostrander, Kelsey Osborne-Garth, and Bailey Oates

awards.title: College of Social Science Provost Undergraduate Research Initiative Award
Outstanding Achievement Award for The Implementation of Creative Projects
